backup.title: Backup & Restore
backup.description: Download a full configuration backup or restore from a previous one.
backup.debugBundleDesc: Download a sanitized bundle for sharing with maintainers. Sensitive fields (API keys, notification credentials, DVR host/port, webhook URLs) are masked. The encryption key and database are excluded.
about.connectDescription: Connect with me and support the project
settings.general.foundServers: Found {count} new server on your network
settings.general.foundServersPlural: Found {count} new servers on your network
settings.general.noNewServers: No new DVR servers found on your network
settings.general.noDvrEmpty: No DVR servers configured. Click “+ Add Server” to add one.
settings.general.timezoneHint: Set the timezone for accurate timestamps in logs and notifications
settings.general.logLevelHint: Control the detail level in application logs
settings.general.logRetentionHint: Automatically delete logs older than specified number of days
settings.general.bgImagesDesc: Artwork shown behind dashboard metric cards
backup.downloadDescPost: , the activity database, all per-DVR session state, and the encryption key in a secured subfolder. Encrypted API keys are preserved as-is — no secrets are decrypted into the archive.
backup.destructiveDescMid: , session state, and the encryption key. A pre-restore snapshot is saved to
backup.destructiveDescPost: before any files are changed. Backups created by a newer version of ChannelWatch are rejected.
backup.uploadDescPost: file previously downloaded from this screen. The archive is validated before anything is written. After a successful restore the core process is hot-reloaded.
security.mode.apiKeyOnly.label: Legacy API key mode
security.mode.apiKeyOnly.summary: This is a legacy install that still relies on a shared API key for browser/API access. New installs should use setup-based secure login or explicit no-auth mode instead.
security.mode.noAuth.label: No authentication
security.mode.noAuth.summary: The UI and API are accessible without credentials. Use only on a trusted private network.
security.mode.rbacWithFallback.label: RBAC + API key fallback
security.mode.rbacWithFallback.summary: Role-based sessions are enabled, but the shared API key still bypasses role checks.
security.mode.rbacOnly.label: RBAC only
security.mode.rbacOnly.summary: Role-based sessions are active and the shared API key fallback is not in play.
security.title: Security
security.description: Live auth state from the backend, showing the saved/configured posture separately from any temporary runtime override.
security.runtimeOverrideSummary: CW_DISABLE_AUTH=true is bypassing authentication for this runtime only. The configured mode is still {label} and will come back after restart without the env override.
security.atRestTitle: Per-DVR API keys are encrypted on disk
security.atRestDesc: The DVR key lives at {path}. New key writes require CHANNELWATCH_SECRET_STORAGE_KEY and use envelope encryption. /config still needs to be protected like credential storage.
security.alert.authBypassedTitle: Temporary runtime auth bypass is active
security.alert.authBypassedDesc: CW_DISABLE_AUTH=true disables the normal API-key and RBAC checks for this running process only. It does not rewrite the saved auth mode, so removing the override restores {label} on restart.
security.alert.rbacFallbackTitle: Legacy API key fallback is still active
security.alert.rbacFallbackDesc: Session logins and role checks are active, but the older shared API key path still works. Keep this only while transitioning older installs.
security.alert.rbacSetupDesc: Create the first admin account through the auth setup flow before depending on session-based sign-in.
security.settingsDescription: Choose the active auth mode and manage the current signed-in account.
security.credentialsUnavailable: Sign in with a session-based account to change username or password from the UI.
security.bootstrapCredsDescription: No-auth mode is saved in settings. Create the first admin account here to switch this install back to secure login.
security.switchToSecureHint: Choose Secure login above to enable the create-admin action.
security.createAdminAndEnable: Create admin and enable secure login
notifications.webhooks.title: Outbound Webhooks
notifications.webhooks.description: Send signed JSON payloads to external receivers for ChannelWatch alert events
notifications.webhooks.alertDescPre: Each enabled webhook gets a POST request with a fixed JSON payload plus
notifications.webhooks.alertDescPost: headers. Leave a masked secret as-is to keep the stored value.
notifications.webhooks.entryDesc: Global destination used for alert deliveries
notifications.webhooks.secretHint: ChannelWatch signs the raw JSON body with HMAC-SHA256 using this secret.
notifications.providers.title: Notification Providers
notifications.templates.title: Notification Templates
notifications.templates.description: Customize title and body templates for each alert family while keeping the current default fallback behavior.
notifications.templates.alertDesc: Leave Use default enabled to keep the built-in formatter output. Turn it off to edit custom text, then use Reset to Default to restore the backend defaults for that alert family.

alerts.streamCounter.desc: Add total active stream count to notifications

alerts.vodWatching.desc: Notifications when recorded content is played
alerts.recordingEvents.title: Recording Events
alerts.recordingEvents.desc: Track recordings from scheduling through completion
alerts.diskSpace.title: Disk Space
alerts.diskSpace.desc: Alert when DVR storage runs low
alerts.enableForDvr: Enable or disable for this DVR
alerts.notifyWhenRecording: Notify me when a recording is...
alerts.diskThreshold: Start disk alerts when free space drops below
alerts.diskAdvancedHint: Need separate warning and critical levels, startup delay, repeat sensitivity, or a test-only route? Open Advanced → Disk Alert Controls.
alerts.diskAdvancedHintDvr: Advanced disk tuning for this DVR lives under Advanced → Disk Alert Controls.
advanced.cache.title: Cache Settings
advanced.cache.desc: How long data is stored before refreshing from DVR
advanced.rate.title: Rate Limiting
advanced.rate.desc: Prevent notification storms across all alert types
advanced.rate.maxNotifDesc: How many before throttling kicks in
advanced.rate.windowDesc: The window the limit applies to
advanced.timing.title: Alert Timing
advanced.timing.desc: Control how often notifications are sent for repeated events
advanced.diskControls.title: Disk Alert Controls
advanced.diskControls.desc: Fine-tune warning and critical levels, repeat behavior, and test routing
advanced.diskControls.hint: Leave the basic disk card for the main low-space trigger. Use these controls when you want separate warning and critical levels, quieter repeat alerts, or a dedicated route for test sends.
advanced.diskControls.testRouteDesc: Optional dedicated provider route used only for disk alert tests
advanced.diskControls.testRoutePlacehold: Leave blank to use the normal provider route
advanced.diskControls.testRouteFallback: If this is empty, disk test alerts fall back to your regular notification provider settings.
advanced.diskControls.testRouteFallbackD: If this override is blank, test alerts fall back to the normal provider settings for this DVR.
advanced.errorReporting.title: Error Reporting (Optional)
advanced.errorReporting.desc: Save a Sentry or GlitchTip DSN for future use; crash reporting is not active in this version.
advanced.errorReporting.dsnDesc: Leave blank to keep unset. The DSN is saved and masked for future support only; no crash reports are sent today.

about.story.heading: Meet Coder Luii:
about.project.intro: ChannelWatch is an advanced monitoring solution designed specifically for Channels DVR users who want real-time insights, notifications, and analytics about their media server.
about.project.detail: Built with a focus on reliability and user experience, ChannelWatch connects your Channels DVR server to the notification providers you already use, with timely alerts and clear monitoring for DVR activity.
about.feature.1: Real-time monitoring of Channels DVR activity
about.feature.2: Customizable notifications for streaming and recording events
about.feature.3: Disk space monitoring with predictive storage forecasting
about.feature.4: Multiple notification platforms support (Discord, Telegram, Email, Pushover)
about.feature.5: Interactive real-time dashboard with 24-hour activity timeline
about.feature.6: Detailed analytics on server performance and usage patterns
about.tech.python.name: Python 3.11+
about.tech.python.desc: Core application logic, API integration, and data processing
about.tech.fastapi.name: FastAPI
about.tech.fastapi.desc: High-performance RESTful API framework with automatic OpenAPI docs
about.tech.react.name: React 18 & Next.js 14
about.tech.react.desc: Frontend framework with server-side rendering for optimal performance
about.tech.docker.name: Docker & Docker Compose
about.tech.docker.desc: Containerization for consistent deployment across environments
about.tech.tailwind.name: Tailwind CSS & shadcn/ui
about.tech.tailwind.desc: Utility-first CSS framework with accessible component library
about.approach.userCentric.title: User-Centric Design
about.approach.userCentric.desc: Built around real user needs with a focus on simplicity and useful information at a glance.
about.approach.apiFirst.title: API-First Approach
about.approach.apiFirst.desc: Clean separation between backend and frontend, with API documentation for operators and integrations.
about.approach.openSource.title: Open Source
about.approach.openSource.desc: Public codebase with issues and discussions for feedback and project support.
supportReport.title: Report a Problem
supportReport.description: Create a sanitized support report preview from this ChannelWatch instance.
supportReport.configError: Report settings could not be loaded.
supportReport.form.summaryPlaceholder: Active Streams shows a stream but no activity or notification appears
supportReport.form.expectedPlaceholder: Describe what you expected ChannelWatch to show or send.
supportReport.form.emailHelp: Email is only used so CoderLuii can follow up with you about this report.
supportReport.form.contactWarningDesc: Without a GetChannels username, GitHub username, or email address, CoderLuii may not be able to notify you about follow-up questions or fixes.
supportReport.form.attachmentsDesc: Attach screenshots that show the problem and a debug bundle ZIP from this instance.
supportReport.form.screenshotsHelp: PNG, JPEG, or WebP. Up to {count} screenshots.
supportReport.form.screenshotsDropDesc: Clipboard images from screen capture tools are accepted.
supportReport.form.screenshotsPasteAnywh: Tip: copy a screenshot, select this screenshot box, then paste.
supportReport.form.debugBundleHelp: Create a fresh bundle now, or attach a previous ChannelWatch debug bundle if it captured the issue earlier.
supportReport.form.attachmentsPrivate: Screenshots and debug bundles go directly to CoderLuii for troubleshooting and are not shown on the report.
supportReport.review.title: Review Report
supportReport.review.privateBoundary: Email and attached files are shared only with CoderLuii for follow-up and troubleshooting.
supportReport.review.noAttachments: No screenshots or debug bundle attached.
supportReport.manual.title: Manual upload fallback
supportReport.manual.description: Use this if direct submit cannot complete, or if this browser cannot reach the upload service. Copy the support code, open the upload portal, and finish from any browser with internet access.
supportReport.manual.privacyNote: The support code carries report text and diagnostics only. Attach screenshots or a debug bundle again on the upload portal, or download the offline package to keep them together.
supportReport.manual.packageDownloadFail: Could not prepare the offline package.
supportReport.success.title: Dry run complete
supportReport.success.description: The report and attachments were validated locally. Nothing was sent.
supportReport.success.liveDescription: The report was submitted. CoderLuii can follow up using the contact details you provided.
supportReport.success.emailTestDescripti: The private report email was sent for testing. No public issue was created.
supportReport.error.summaryMax: Problem summary must be 500 characters or less.
supportReport.error.expectedMax: Expected behavior must be 2000 characters or less.
supportReport.error.getchannels: Use letters, numbers, underscores, dots, or hyphens.
supportReport.error.payloadTooLarge: This report is larger than the configured size limit.
supportReport.error.attachmentsDisabled: Attachments are not enabled for this report endpoint.
supportReport.error.attachmentTooLarge: Each attachment must be {size} or smaller.
supportReport.error.totalAttachmentsTooL: All attachments together must be {size} or smaller.
supportReport.error.screenshotType: Screenshots must be PNG, JPEG, or WebP files.
supportReport.error.debugBundleType: Debug bundle must be a ZIP file.
supportReport.error.debugBundleInvalid: Attach a ChannelWatch-generated debug bundle ZIP.
supportReport.error.debugBundleCreate: Could not create the debug bundle.
